ui_texts.title: PERIODIC TABLE OF ELEMENTS
ui_texts.search_placeholder: Search by name, symbol or atomic number...
ui_texts.molar_prompt: Enter a chemical formula (e.g. H2O, Ca(OH)2) to calculate its molar mass.
ui_texts.stoichiometry_prompt: Enter a chemical equation (e.g. Fe + O2 -> Fe2O3) to balance it.
ui_texts.lewis_prompt: Select an element to view its Lewis dot diagram.
ui_texts.lewis_switch_prompt: Switch to the Lewis panel to see the dot diagram.
ui_texts.lewis_not_applicable: Lewis dot diagrams are not applicable for transition metals, lanthanides, or actinides.
ui_texts.solubility_prompt: Select a cation and anion, then click Check.
ui_texts.solubility_rule_alkali: Alkali metals and ammonium compounds are always soluble.
ui_texts.solubility_rule_nitrate_acetate: All nitrates and acetates are soluble.
ui_texts.solubility_rule_halide: Most chlorides, bromides, and iodides are soluble.
ui_texts.solubility_rule_carbonate_phosphate_sulf: Most carbonates, phosphates, and sulfides are insoluble.
ui_texts.solubility_rule_default: No specific rule applies; assumed insoluble.
ui_texts.info_prompt: Select an element to see its details.
ui_texts.compound_prompt: Choose one element at a time, send it to A or B, then choose the oxidation states and calculate one simple binary formula.
ui_texts.diagram_prompt: Select an element to display the orbital diagram.
ui_texts.diagram_switch_prompt: Select the 'Electron configuration' view to display the orbital diagram of the selected element.
ui_texts.no_common_compounds: No curated common compounds are stored for this pair yet.
ui_texts.pair_ready_prompt: A and B are ready. Select oxidation states to calculate one simple binary formula, or read the curated compounds listed below.
ui_texts.must_select_ab: You must select both A and B before combining the elements.
ui_texts.same_element: The current builder does not support combining an element with itself yet.
ui_texts.select_oxidation: Select oxidation states for both elements.
ui_texts.opposite_sign: The current builder only supports combinations with opposite-sign oxidation states.
ui_texts.more_info: Overview based on the data currently available for this element.
ui_texts.name: Name
ui_texts.about_description: Desktop reference app for exploring element data, electron configuration, and a limited binary compound builder.
ui_texts.search_helper: Search by name, symbol, or atomic number.
ui_texts.builder_selection_hint: Pick one element at a time, then send it to A or B.
ui_texts.quick_help_body:
- Search by name, symbol, or atomic number, then press Enter or pick a suggestion.
- Use the language selector to switch the UI language.
- Use the Info, Electron config., and Compounds panels to inspect the selected element.
- In the compound builder, select two elements, choose one oxidation state for each, then calculate one simple binary formula.
ui_texts.current_limits_body:
- The compound builder is limited to simple binary compounds with opposite-sign oxidation states, and the nomenclature shown only covers the currently implemented paths.
- Some scientific fields can still be missing or incomplete for some elements.
- Visible UI languages follow the audited localizations currently bundled with the app.
ui_texts.compound_scope_note: Scope: simple binary compounds only. Same-element pairs and same-sign oxidation states are not supported yet.
ui_texts.scientific_data_partial_note: Dataset note: some scientific values are currently unavailable for this element: {fields}.
ui_texts.scientific_data_partial_note_more: Dataset note: some scientific values are currently unavailable for this element, including {fields}, and {count} more.
